summary: Full-stack testing of rebuilt Student Ride History page. Backend: 19/19 pytest tests passed after fixing one Postgres bug in cancel-ride endpoint. Frontend: All critical flows verified for Priya (5 rides) and Liam (4 rides) — topbar correctly simplified, sidebar active state, summary cards exact values match, tabs/search/sort/reset work, View modal, Receipt modal (CBS-2026-000020, $9.50, Download triggers HTML file), active banner only for Priya, mobile <900px shows cards and hides table, cross-user isolation enforced. Profile and Need Help pages still render correctly with the new simplified topbar.

issues: 'Campus Bike Sharing' brand text wraps to 3 lines on narrow mobile widths, slightly squashing the topbar height. Consider hiding 'Bike Sharing' or shrinking brand on very small viewports.
In-browser Babel transformer is still used (console warning) — same as previous iteration. Pre-compile for production.

action_items: Re-seed Liam's upcoming ride (ride_id 28) if you want the upcoming-tab UI flow to be testable again — backend cancel test consumed it (per the request, this was expected).
Consider extracting requireUser middleware shared across student.js / profile.js / support.js / bookings.js (DRY).
Remove the hardcoded JWT_SECRET fallback 'change-me-to-a-long-random-string' in student.js (and other route files) and require the env var to fail fast.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED: backend/routes/student.js POST /api/student/rides/:id/cancel raised Postgres error 'could not determine data type of parameter $2' because CONCAT() couldn't infer type — fixed by casting $2::text. Was producing HTTP 400 with that error on any cancel attempt.
DRY violation: requireUser middleware copy-pasted into student.js (also exists in profile.js, support.js, bookings.js). Extract to /app/backend/middleware/requireUser.js.
JWT_SECRET fallback ('change-me-to-a-long-random-string') is hardcoded — security risk. Should throw at startup if env missing.

student.js: bucketize() treats 'pending' rides whose start_time is in the past as 'active', which can mis-classify abandoned bookings (e.g., a stale pending ride past its start window is reported as active). Consider an explicit expired bucket or comparing against expires_at.
test_credentials: Priya: priya.student@uni.edu.au / TestPass123! and Liam: liam.chen@uni.edu.au / Student2024! (both from /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: Cancelled Liam's previously-upcoming ride (id 28) via backend test (intentional per request). Liam now has Completed=2, Active=0, Upcoming=0, Cancelled=2 instead of the original 1 cancelled.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 critical Ride-History endpoints are working: list+summary, single, receipt (only for completed), cancel (12+ char reason, type cast bug fixed). Liam's upcoming ride was consumed by the backend cancel test — re-seed if upcoming-flow UI re-test needed. Topbar simplified across all 3 student pages and verified. Tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_student_ride_history.py.

verified_acceptance_criteria.summary_cards_priya: PASS (Completed=3, Upcoming=0, Active=1, Spend=$36.90, CO2=3.71kg, Distance=19.3km)
verified_acceptance_criteria.active_banner_priya_only: PASS (banner+End Ride for Priya, absent for Liam)
verified_acceptance_criteria.tabs_filter_table: PASS (All=5, Upcoming=0, Active=1, Completed=3, Cancelled=1 for Priya)

rca_of_the_issue: POST /api/student/rides/:id/cancel was crashing with 'could not determine data type of parameter $2'. Root cause: in PostgreSQL, when a parameter is used solely inside CONCAT() with another untyped expression, the driver can't infer its data type. Reproduction: any valid cancel request with a 12+ char reason returned HTTP 400 with that error. Fix: cast `$2::text` in the UPDATE statement (student.js line 206). Verified by re-running the test class — all 3 cancel tests now pass.
